text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** Our {days|日々} are filled with lots of things to do.
**DrJill:** Some things we do every day, like {help|手伝う} around the house.
**DrJill:** {brush|歯を磨く/髪をとかす} our hair, eat dinner.
**DrJill:** Some things we have to do.
**DrJill:** So we write them down.
**DrJill:** What do you do in a day?
**DrJill:** Let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She {answers|答える} the question.
**DrJill:** What do you do in a day?
**Anna:** Hi there.
**Anna:** Welcome back.
**Max:** Anna, I have our question.
**Max:** What do you do in a day?
**Anna:** Right on time, Max.
**Anna:** In my {day planner|スケジュール帳}, it says 9 a.
**Anna:** m.
**Anna:** Get question.
**Max:** A day planner is {helpful|役立つ} and important.
**Anna:** In my day planner is my {to-do list|やることリスト}.
**Anna:** On my to-do list, I write the things I do every day.
**Max:** Did you write?
**Max:** Brush my teeth?
**Anna:** No, Max.
**Max:** Did you write eat breakfast?
**Anna:** No, Max.
**Anna:** Those are things I do every day.
**Anna:** So, I do not write them on my to-do list.
**Anna:** On my {to-do list|やるべきことリスト}/タスクリスト/, I write things I need to do.
**Anna:** Like.
**Anna:** {Clean|掃除する} my clothes and {hang|干す} them.
**Anna:** {Sweep|掃く} the floor.
**Anna:** Wash the car.
**Anna:** I want to go to the park today.
**Anna:** Daisy, I am {busy|忙しい} today.
**Anna:** So we cannot go to the park.
**Anna:** I have an idea.
**Anna:** I can help you.
**Anna:** Thanks, Daisy.
**Anna:** But you cannot do my work.
**Anna:** Your {day planner|スケジュール帳} and to-do list are gone.
**DrJill:** So we can go to the park.
**Max:** Oh, Daisy.
**DrJill:** English has some small but helpful words.
**DrJill:** Anna, Max, and Daisy just used all of them.
**DrJill:** Let's see them again.
**DrJill:** The small, helpful words are and, but, and so.
**DrJill:** and joins two or more things that go together or happen together.
**Max:** A {day planner|スケジュール帳} is helpful and {important|重要な}.
**DrJill:** But joins two thoughts that are different or opposite.
**Anna:** Thanks, Daisy.
**Anna:** But you cannot do
**Anna:** My work.
**DrJill:** So shows one thing causing another thing to happen.
**Anna:** Daisy, I am busy today.
**Anna:** So we cannot go to the park, but and so
**Anna:** They are small words, but they are important and common.
**Anna:** So you need to learn them.
**Max:** Anna, you said {them|それら/それらのもの|/ðɛm/|I gave them to her.} all.
**Anna:** Fill in the {blank|空欄/空白|/blæŋk/|Please fill in the blank.} with and but or so.
**Anna:** I like to {wash|洗う/洗濯する|/wɒʃ/|I wash my car every week.} dishes
**Anna:** I do not like to {sweep|掃く/掃除する|/swiːp/|I sweep the floor every day.} the floor.
**Anna:** I like to wash dishes, but I do not like to sweep the floor.
**Anna:** I like to {help|助ける/手伝う|/help/|Can you help me?} around the house.
**Anna:** I clean my room.
**Anna:** I like to help around the house.
**Anna:** So I clean my room.
**Anna:** Now let's hear from some {friends|友人/仲間|/frɛndz/|My friends are coming over.}.
**Anna:** They talk about their {days|日/一日|/deɪz/|I have busy days.}.
**Anna:** In the morning I get {dressed|着替える/服を着る|/drɛst/|I dressed quickly.} and brush my teeth.
**Students:** In the morning I {met|会った/出会う|/mɛt/|I met my friend.} my bed.
**Students:** In the afternoon, I do my {homework|宿題|/ˈhoʊmwɜːrk/|I finish my homework early.}.
**DrJill:** Anna's day {planner|手帳/スケジュール帳|/ˈplænər/|I use a planner.} and her to-do list are gone, but she still has to do the work.
**DrJill:** So, what is going to {happen|起こる/発生する|/ˈhæpən/|What will happen next?}.
**Anna:** Daisy, I was wrong.
**Anna:** You can do my work.
**Anna:** I am {helping|手伝っている/助けている|/ˈhɛlpɪŋ/|I am helping my friend.}, but I do not like it.
**DrJill:** Now you can talk about the things you do in a day.
**DrJill:** And come back again to Let's Learn English with Anna.
